title: Probability & Counting
blurb: Probability rules, Venn diagrams, tree diagrams, contingency tables, the fundamental counting principle and arrangements with restrictions.

questions.context: For two events $A$ and $B$ it is given that $P(A)=0{,}35$, $P(B)=0{,}5$ and $P(A\text{ or }B)=0{,}675$.

parts.q: Are the events $A$ and $B$ independent? Motivate your answer with a calculation.
solution: Since $P(A)\times P(B)=P(A\text{ and }B)=0{,}175$, the events ARE independent

parts.q: Determine the probability that neither $A$ nor $B$ occurs.

questions.context: In a group of $120$ Grade 12 learners at a school in Tshwane, $65$ take Mathematics (M), $50$ take Physical Sciences (P) and $25$ take both subjects. The rest take neither subject.

parts.q: Draw a Venn diagram to represent this information, showing the number of learners in each region.
parts.answer: Regions: only M $=40$, both $=25$, only P $=25$, neither $=30$

parts.q: Determine the probability that a randomly chosen learner takes neither of the two subjects.
Are the events M and P mutually exclusive? Give a reason for your answer.
solution: Since learners CAN take both subjects, the events are NOT mutually exclusive

questions.context: Lerato travels to school either by taxi or on foot. The probability that she takes a taxi is $0{,}6$. If she takes a taxi, the probability that she is late is $0{,}1$. If she walks, the probability that she is late is $0{,}3$.

parts.q: Draw a tree diagram to represent this situation. Show the probability on each branch.
solution: From Taxi: Late ($0{,}1$) and Not late ($0{,}9$)
From Walk: Late ($0{,}3$) and Not late ($0{,}7$)

parts.q: Calculate the probability that Lerato is late for school on a given day.
Calculate the probability that Lerato is NOT late for school.

questions.context: A spaza shop owner in Soweto recorded how $150$ customers paid and whether they bought airtime. Of the $90$ customers who paid cash, $45$ bought airtime. Of the $60$ customers who paid by card, $25$ bought airtime. (Contingency table totals: cash $=90$, card $=60$; airtime $=70$, no airtime $=80$.)

parts.q: Determine the probability that a randomly selected customer paid cash.
Determine the probability that a randomly selected customer paid cash AND bought airtime.
Are the events 'paid cash' and 'bought airtime' independent? Motivate your answer with calculations.
solution: $0{,}28\ne 0{,}3$, so the events are NOT independent

parts.q: Determine the probability that a randomly selected customer paid cash OR bought airtime.

questions.context: Consider the letters of the word MOSSEL (as in Mossel Bay). Note that the letter S appears twice.

parts.q: In how many different ways can all six letters be arranged in a row?
In how many of these arrangements does the word begin AND end with the letter S?
solution: Place the two S's in the first and last positions (identical letters: 1 way)
Arrange the remaining 4 different letters in the middle: $4!=24$

parts.q: If one of the $360$ arrangements is chosen at random, determine the probability that it begins and ends with the letter S.

A security gate code consists of $3$ different letters of the alphabet followed by $2$ digits (digits MAY repeat). Using the fundamental counting principle, determine how many different codes are possible.
